title: Chapter Ninety-One: The Prostration
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He sat across from Azure Qilin and studied the man who would kill him, or die trying.

Namgung Se-jin was approaching thirty. Handsome, with gently downcast eyes and lips that held a quiet resolve. He wore deep blue silk -- darker than Desolate Fortress robes -- and it sat on him like armour, like skin. The young master of the Namgung family. They said he had been the foremost rising talent in South Zhili. That was a story of the past. These days no one spoke of him among the young. They spoke of his brother instead.

*White Qilin's brother.*

A Namgung swordswoman with long hair brought tea. Her movements were careful despite the stern energy rolling off her -- a warrior playing maid, and volunteering for it.

"Thank you, Yeon Mae," Se-jin said.

She answered with a small smile and took position at the doorway like a sentinel. The guest house was already ringed with Namgung warriors. They guarded this man as though he were a king.

*They say they are royalty in South Zhili.* The rumours felt immediately true.

"I'm glad to see you look well," Se-jin said. He was watching Hwa-shin with gentle eyes. "Desolate Fortress has strict laws. I worried constantly. But the air of Xiangyang seems to suit you. You've become very strong."

"Thanks to you, elder brother."

"I may have contributed a little." A playful smile. "You don't know how much scolding I took from the elders. A month in seclusion, for helping you leave. The Emperor Sword Form is still difficult study."

"I'm sorry." Hwa-shin bowed his head. A different posture than the one he wore before Namgung Mi. Genuine.

Yeon-shin watched from beside them and thought it seemed reasonable on the surface.

Se-jin said: "I understand why you volunteered for the martial matches. Since preventing the event was impossible, you meant to conclude matters yourself. I'm sorry. And worried."

"Brother..."

"It's karmic retribution for the main family." A murmur. Then the young master turned his head. "And you are..."

His gaze approached. Intense. Yeon-shin did not look away. He met it steadily.

"Desolate Fortress' Lightning Genius. We've met before. The yin techniques you revealed at Clear Night Valley were very impressive."

"Let me speak of Namgung Mi."

Se-jin shook his head. "The evil sect master who suddenly appeared? It's fine. Though she's my sister, Mi has accumulated much karma. As the main family's young master, I investigated the Virtuous Teacher Sect matter in Wuchang. She handled it her own way. As her brother, I can only deeply regret not guiding her properly."

"Elder brother!" Namgung Mi's voice rang sharp from the guest room. Se-jin closed and opened his eyes once. Ignoring her.

The spirit light burning in both his eyes was very distinct.

"I too am petty," he said quietly, looking at Yeon-shin. "Narrow capacity, like our elders. Even seeing you today, I've been worrying. But this is indeed the right thing to do."

"What do you mean?"

"I should have begged you first."

He stood. Stepped to the side of the table. Then his body slowly tilted forward. Hands extended. Waist bending. His upper body came down and did not stop. Gasps from every corner of the room.

The Namgung family young master made a full prostration to a sixteen-year-old boy from an enemy sect.

"An apology that must be made," he said from the lowest position. "My deep gratitude for your support of Namgung at Clear Night Valley. An apology from the young master who repaid grace with betrayal. And the self-consolation of a small man who wishes to face martial matches with you even slightly honourably."

Silence. Heavy as stone.

*This person's sword techniques must be frightening.*

Yeon-shin showed nothing on his face. But inwardly he was perplexed. He had not expected to find the scent of chivalry in a great family. He knew even less that his life-and-death opponent would be such a man.

"I dare not seek forgiveness," Se-jin said. "The martial matches are set. Just accept this."

He did not raise his head for a long time.

"You're right," Yeon-shin said at last. "The life-and-death match is decided. One of us must die, so there's no point discussing meaning. I'll consider the young master's apology received. Rise, and speak of business."

"Truly. Thank you."

Se-jin rose and dusted his seat. His face wore a sheepish smile. Even his mind was polished. The man before him had an unyielding inner self -- chilling, yet curious.

*What kind of sword path does he possess?*

---

They left Huangzhou as four. Se-jin had dismissed all his warriors and insisted his sister walk with them. "This could be your last murim journey," he told her. "We siblings haven't even toured the world together."

"What do you mean *last*--!"

"Follow me."

So four walked the forest path. Gold-shimmering dirt under eight footprints. Perhaps ten, if the Seventh Apostle still followed.

It was a number hard to walk together amicably. One by one, they had to die.

Only Se-jin's weighty humour occasionally scattered the dust. "Lightning Genius and White Qilin," he said. "Two names spreading beyond Hubei. If you survive, cherish your friendship. Worthy opponents forge each other into divine swords. For me it was the Hwangbo family's Lazy Flame Dragon, but they say he's locked in the thunder prison now."

"Who knows if he's still alive," Yeon-shin answered.

"Though he seemed carefree, he was a foolish friend." Se-jin smiled bitterly. "He wanted to break free from his family but it ended with just thoughts. Indeed, murim matters are hard to predict."

That was when twenty armed men burst from the trees behind them.

"Is Desolate Fortress' Lightning Genius there!" A voice loaded with internal force. Closer to an announcement than a question.

"Endure the young master of Deep Martial Alliance's punishment!"

The leader sprang forward with his sword already drawn. Not asking right from wrong.

*Deep Martial Alliance.* Yeon-shin recalled the murim journey where he had beheaded Sword Art Flower. Ma Jin had warned him. Guard against grudges. The betrothed would come.

He grabbed his hilt and answered the charge.

The blades met. Tremendous sword force spread to the back of his hand. Beyond ordinary.

"Young master?" Yeon-shin said.

"I am the Hundred Man Commander! The young master sent me!"

*So you're not him.*

And then something else happened. Something behind the charging men.

Overwhelming presence. The air itself seemed to evacuate. The Deep Martial Alliance warriors' movements slowed -- then stopped entirely. Twenty bodies frozen, trapped like flies in amber.

Emperor Sword Form.

Se-jin stood among them, alone and noble, drawing his brilliant sword. He walked the forest path. He began cutting the necks of frozen enemies one by one.

Dignity in his leisurely gait. One death with each breath. Life threads cut alongside despair-filled exhalations.

While the young master executed his divine technique, Yeon-shin gripped the Hundred Man Commander's sword with one hand and placed his palm against the man's abdomen. Demon Roaring Blood Technique and Demon Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm surged as one.

The explosion tore through the forest. Trees shook. Birds screamed skyward.

Yeon-shin turned while shaking blood from his hand.

Se-jin raised his gaze. His eyes asked the question.

"I have shown you," Yeon-shin said.

The young master understood. The boy had engaged in psychological warfare. Deliberately revealing his incomplete technique. The martial match had already begun.

And Se-jin had shown his Emperor Sword Form.

*We are even.*

---
